reviews.review_msg: Eva is a good person and seems to enjoy what she's teaching. However, she's also unorganized and keeps changing what she says. For exam 2, for example, there were parts that she had mentioned that won't be on the exam. Even after telling her, she didn't seem to care much. I supposed where I actually learned a lot was during the tutorials with TA.

Very nice, prof you can tell she cares about her students and is very passionate, though 3 hours is a long lecture and is hard to get through. Her exams are quite difficult I found so make sure to study a ton but she does give bonus marks. Keep in mind that she does a lecture before the exam and that content will be on the exam.

Makes no sense in the lectures; she goes on and on with examples, but doesn't actually teach the concept. I can tell she's passionate, but she isn't great at expressing it. Her time management is not great either, made 2 assignments due the night before our final, which makes no sense as professors should give time to students to study on their own

She likes to get to know your name- that's AWESOME. But... the class material is difficult to understand as there are only a few words on slides. Lots of charts. Read the textbook for the class as it's helpful!!
Her exams are bad and her lectures are boring, her ratings should not be this high

Eva is super passionate about the topic and really wants her students to succeed. Her 3 hour lectures can feel a bit long, and while the homework practice is helpful, I do wish there was a clear answer key. Her exams are quite difficult despite being mostly m/c - make sure you really study and attend office hours if you're confused.
lectures were clear and the tests were not unreasonable

She's very kind but also very disorganized and not good at explaining. She often misunderstands questions and doesn't answer properly. I've had her for ling 200 and 323. They were both 3 hour long lectures and it was excruciating to sit through, didn't feel like I was learning much. homework is easy and for completion but you don't rly get feedback

Dr. Tchizmarova was a great! I took this class as an elective and didn't know a lot about linguistics, yet she made everything super easy to understand and all of her lectures were super engaging. Tests were moderately hard because they included memorizing super specific details from textbook examples, but other than that the class was great!
Truly an amazing instructor, would definitely take another class with her, will easily recommend to anyone thinking about taking a course with her !

Her exams are stupidly hard and there's a bunch of group assignments so you better pray you get a good group
Her exams were a total pain and she was kinda strict but she was a nice person

The professor is a nice person but not an effective instructor. Exams don't reflect what's taught—she tests on things mentioned once and not found in slides or the textbook, yet expects detailed answers. Assignment criteria are unclear, and she gets defensive when asked about grading. Not a GPA booster do not take this course if you can avoid it.

Grossly long lectures and projects that make you want to rip your hair out. Multiple choice exams that refer to something mentioned briefly in lectures and randomly assigned group projects in which your group mates decide whether or not you get a good grade. Takes away marks often and inconsistently. 3 hour lectures that melt your brain.

Lectures were fine, easy enough, but group projects are her entire assignment method for this class and 160 which sucks. Tutorials are mandatory and annoying cuz the TA's have a power trip so no one likes to respond. Fairly good class if you're okay with cold calls in tutorials and just focus on the material yourself.

Eva is very good at teaching the introductory course; she keeps in mind that the amount of concepts covered can be confusing for non-linguistics students. Very warm and wants to help her students succeed. I didn't enjoy tutorials because the participation model was terrible/borderline humiliating, but it depends on the TA. Prof is wonderful though!

She was so lovely, taking another class with her next semester! Lecture isn't mandatory per se, but if you want to understand the content well then highly recommend you attend. She's very interactive and provides a lot of examples which helps a lot with your understanding.

Eva is lovely, but this course can be a grind. Weekly assignments aren't terrible, but they get more complex and confusing over time. 2 group projects: should be okay if you have a solid group. Exams are tough as nails no matter how much you study, as the content is pretty dense. Love the prof, not so much the course. Take it only if necessary!
She is relatable and very enthusiastic when teaching linguistic course! Wonderful prof
Class material is interesting and makes sense. Exams were unnecessary difficult. Only take if you need this class and are ready to put in hours to keep up.

Dr. Eva is awesome! She takes into consideration that since the course has no pre reqs, there are people in class with no LING background whatsoever. The course requires constant work, so do no fall behind! She directs her coursework in a way that in the end everything fits nicely into a puzzle. Would definitely take another LING course with her.
